generated_comment: Honestly if she knew he was married and still kept getting close, then what else to call it? Total ghar todne wali behaviour only. Men are stupid sometimes, but she should have had some basic sense and stayed away.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman for an affair while minimizing the man's responsibility ('men are stupid sometimes'). It encodes homewrecker framing, seductress logic, and family-protection rhetoric.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 19. A college student in Chennai. You are in your college's unofficial girls WhatsApp group. Someone has posted about a female classmate named Divya who is always seen with the boys in the class Ñ she eats with them, studies with them, hangs out with them. The group has been discussing Divya for the past hour. You are one of the people commenting. What you say about Divya and why you say it and what words you use and what conclusion the group reaches about what kind of girl Divya is Ñ all of this is the content of this persona.
